criteriaCoverage.criterion: Each node type opens a working editor that persists its config into the definition and round-trips; the IF editor reuses the segment rule builder and compiles to a valid AstNode; the SEND editor clones+edits a per-node email copy (envelope) reusing the broadcast flow.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: NodeEditor.tsx has one focused editor per type (trigger/wait/wait-until/hour-window/condition/send/update-profile/webhook/exit) dispatched by NodeEditorBody. Pure serializers in node-config.ts round-trip each (node-config.test.ts 17 + node-config.serialize.test.ts 3 green; full-definition round-trips identity + passes validateAutomationDefinition). IF + event-filter editors mount the SAME shared web/src/segments/RuleBuilder.tsx extracted from SegmentBuilder; writeConditionConfig compiles via buildAstFromGroup → validateAst (no second AST path). SEND editor calls POST /automations/:id/send-nodes/:nodeId/attach-template to clone a kind='copy' and repoints template_id, then onReloadAutomation before close; Design email sets editorReturn=/automations/:id (createAs:'copy' for blank). Integration test automation-node-editors (6, real PG): every-node-type byte-for-byte PUT→GET round-trip, attach-template clones a kind=copy + repoints, set_attribute expression+legacy-scalar persist. Chromium: node-editors 8/8 incl. SEND attach+Design-email return (155), IF same-rule-builder+empty-group-block (84), webhook write-only-secret (106), update-profile literal↔expression (134) — all PASS.

criteriaCoverage.criterion: Publish is blocked (with specific, inline reasons) when the graph is invalid or a send node lacks From/To/Subject or the trigger is incomplete; it succeeds once everything is valid. Verified in a REAL browser per node type.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: activateAutomation now validates the stored definition (validateDefinitionOr400 → typed 400, status stays draft) BEFORE the envelope gate; the 409 carries {error, node, missing} (sender→to→subject ordered) then the verified-domain gate with missing:'verified_domain'. automation-activate-gate.integration.test.ts (5, real PG): missing-sender 409 {node:'send',missing:'sender'}, to/subject discriminators, verified_domain, structurally-invalid stored def → 400 not 500 (status draft), incomplete event trigger (no eventType) → 400 not 500. AutomationBuilder.publish persists-then-activates, renders publish-reason + per-node publish-errors keyed by body.node, flips status→active on 200. ApiError now spreads the full body so node/missing survive (client.ts). Chromium automation-publish-gate 2/2: complete journey → active; no-From blocks inline (publish-reason + node-publish-error, status stays draft) then publishes after fixing envelope. browserVerified.

criteriaCoverage.criterion: All server-calling buttons spinner+lock; no native dialogs; data-testid on every control; workspace-scoped throughout.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: Every save/attach/publish Button uses onClick={fn} returning the promise → kit Button auto-locks (verified in NodeEditor.tsx node-save/send-attach-template/send-design-email and AutomationBuilder automation-publish/save-automation). grep for confirm(/alert(/prompt( across editors/node-config/AutomationBuilder/RuleBuilder = NONE (only a comment 'never window.confirm'); blocks use inline hints (condition-incomplete, update-incomplete, webhook-error) + showToast. 47 data-testids in NodeEditor; RuleBuilder preserves all rule-row/rule-field/rule-operator/rule-value/add-rule etc. Chromium spec 'no native dialogs are used anywhere in the editors' PASS. Handlers scope via scopedQuery on ctx.workspaceId; cross-workspace template_id and trigger_segment_id rejected (404/400), proven in integration tier.

recommendations: The webhook editor's secret-header detection is a regex heuristic (SECRET_HEADER_RE matching authorization/x-*-secret|token|key/api-key). A user-named custom auth header outside that pattern would be stored/round-tripped as a plain header (its value echoed back on reopen). Low risk for this UI/validation-only phase, but when phase-7 actually EXECUTES webhooks, move secret handling to an explicit user-marked field + the @cdp/db secret-crypto envelope rather than a name pattern.

Webhook SSRF/domain-allowlist and bounded-retry ENFORCEMENT is out of scope here (this phase is editor + client/server URL validation only — http(s) protocol check). Confirm phase-7 (webhook execution) adds the per-workspace domain allowlist + loopback/link-local/169.254 blocking + injected HTTP client + bounded retries before any real outbound call ships.

SendEditor.senderName() renders a static 'a named sender' label rather than the real sender name/email; consider resolving sender_id → display name in the instance card so the envelope summary is informative.

Consider an integration/unit assertion that the webhook node's persisted secret survives a save where the secret field was left blank (the existingSecretHeaders preservation path in writeWebhookConfig) — it's covered by the e2e 'never echoed' check but a direct unit test would lock the write-only round-trip.
